channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Indonesia’s free meals programme plagued by alleged corruption and waste
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's initiative has been criticised for misdirecting resources to affluent regions.

item.title: Wildcard Fery faces Zverev in Wimbledon semis, as Kostyuk meets Noskova
item.description: A partisan Centre Court backed home hope and world number 114 Fery in his incredible fairytale run to the last four.

item.title: US expands military strikes on Iran after Trump says he’ll ‘hit them hard’
item.description: US forces have expanded their military strikes on Iran, after blaming Tehran for breaking the MoU to end the war.

item.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira, BTS in World Cup final halftime show
item.description: FIFA expects 'a couple of billion' to tune in to the 11-minute performance, the first at a World Cup.

item.title: Family demands investigation after US man killed by ICE agent in Texas
item.description: Calls for answers after Lorenzo Salgado Araujo fatally shot during traffic stop in Houston in latest ICE killing.

item.title: US moves to delist Syria as a ‘state sponsor of terrorism’
item.description: President Trump announces a move to delist Syria, citing efforts by Ahmed al-Sharaa to unify the country.

item.title: Former Wisconsin judge avoids prison in ICE obstruction case
item.description: Hannah Dugan's sentence comes after she helped an immigrant in the US avoid ICE agents waiting to arrest him in 2025.

item.title: France lose appeal against Olise’s yellow card in Paraguay World Cup clash
item.description: If Olise is cautioned again against Morocco, he will be suspended for a potential semifinal against Spain or Belgium.

item.title: Deadly strikes in Gaza leave 5 dead, children wounded
item.description: Israeli strikes in Khan Yunis and Gaza City killed five people and wounded others, including children.

item.title: Former rebel turned government ally proposes a path to peace for Sudan
item.description: Malik Agar Ayyir has proposed a national dialogue to end the civil war and steer Sudan towards democratic civilian rule.

item.title: Trump departs NATO summit on older Air Force One
item.description: Trump departed the NATO summit on an older Air Force One after arriving aboard the new Qatar-gifted aircraft.

item.title: Democrat Rahm Emanuel tells Israel to no longer expect unconditional US aid
item.description: Former Obama official is expected to run for president in 2028, with speech reflecting shifting Democratic sentiments.

item.title: US says conducting new wave of strikes on Iran as ceasefire falters
item.description: Second night of strikes comes after Trump says ceasefire 'over', suggests talks for lasting peace will continue.

item.title: Venezuelan fashion designer swaps dresses for body bags
item.description: A Venezuelan fashion designer has turned his workshop from making colourful dresses to producing body bags.

item.title: US summer travel slumps under rising fares and fuel expenses
item.description: US summer trips decline as 45 percent of Americans skip holidays due to soaring airfares and car travel expenses.

item.title: Marine Le Pen cleared to run in France’s 2027 Presidential election
item.description: A French court ruling has reopened Marine Le Pen’s path to the 2027 presidential election.

item.title: Trump ordered to pay E Jean Carroll $5.8m after failed appeal
item.description: Trump ordered to pay $5.8m after the US Supreme Court rejected his appeal in the Carroll defamation and abuse case.

item.title: Search under way for crew of Pakistani cargo plane that crashed into sea
item.description: Rescuers have located submerged wreckage from the aircraft that disappeared as it approached Karachi.

item.title: Five key takeaways from the NATO summit in Ankara
item.description: Trump dominates summit with anti-Europe and Iran diatribes as NATO pledges to expand defence budgets and Ukraine support

item.title: Argentina fans accused of violence, racism by Egypt, Cape Verde supporters
item.description: Fans tell Al Jazeera Argentina supporters hurled beer bottles and abuse at them when their team scored in knockout games.

item.title: Mass cleanup underway after severe flooding in China
item.description: Residents and volunteers in China’s Guangxi province have begun clearing up their flooded homes.

item.title: Trump says US ‘may take over Kharg Island’: Here’s what you need to know
item.description: Kharg Island, pivotal to Iran's oil exports, becomes focal point as Trump renews threats amid rising Gulf tensions.

item.title: War on Gaza draws large crowds to Khamenei’s funeral in Iraq
item.description: More than a million mourners packed streets in Najaf, Iraq for Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei’s funeral procession.

item.title: Israeli air strikes in Gaza kill eight, including two children
item.description: Medics report 12 injuries alongside eight deaths in Gaza as Israeli air strikes target civilians and displaced families.

item.title: Trump says he ‘may be gone’ as he declares himself Iran’s No. 1 target
item.description: US President Donald Trump declared himself Iran’s ‘No. 1 target’ after claiming their leaders have been eliminated
